nodes.name: Config
nodes.notes: Non-secret config + AliExpress app credentials. SECRET-IN-FILE TRADEOFF: aliAppKey / aliAppSecret / aliTrackingId live here because the signing Code nodes cannot read the credentials manager and $env is unavailable on this n8n plan (same tradeoff as the SP-API variant lwa* keys). Treat them as rotatable secrets. OpenRouter / S3 / Postgres keys stay in their credentials. Default gateway api-sg.aliexpress.com/sync expects epoch-ms timestamps (aliTimestampFormat=ms) + sha256 (HMAC) signing; switch to md5 / datetime if the gateway rejects the signature. Tune aliCategoryIds / aliKeywords / aliSort to target deals.

nodes.name: Fetch Categories
nodes.notes: One row per category. Reused via $('Fetch Categories').all() in Build Classifier Request.

nodes.name: Fetch Subcategories
nodes.notes: NEW: Loads subcategories table so AI-generated subcategory name can be resolved to subcategory_id (uuid). Adjust column names if schema differs.

nodes.name: Loop Over Items
nodes.notes: Output 0 = done (end of run). Output 1 = loop body, processes one ASIN at a time.

nodes.name: Normalise
nodes.notes: AliExpress affiliate product → products shape. affiliate_source='aliexpress'; affiliate_url = tracked promotion_link (falls back to product_detail_url, logged). condition='New'; seller from shop name when present; rating from evaluate_rate %. Same skip-and-continue + first-valid-price fallback as eBay; emits the asin alias so the reused spine works unchanged.

nodes.name: Is Valid?
nodes.notes: v3.1: replaces the old Filter (which DROPPED invalid items and stalled the loop — the loop-back edge never fired, halting the run). TRUE (valid) → Check Existing AI Fields. FALSE (skipReason set) → Log Skipped Item → back to Loop Over Items, so the queue always advances.

nodes.name: Log Skipped Item
nodes.notes: v3.1: NEW. Structured 'item_skipped' log (asin + reason + timestamp) for auditing, then loops back to Loop Over Items.

parameters.query:
-- v3.2: single-row seed LEFT JOIN-ed to products so this ALWAYS returns exactly
-- one row. A new product (no match) yields found=false and every has_*=false —
-- previously WHERE slug=$1 returned 0 rows, the node emitted no items, and the
-- whole loop body halted at this node.
SELECT
  (p.slug IS NOT NULL)                                                       AS found,
  COALESCE(p.subcategory_id IS NOT NULL, false)                              AS has_subcategory_id,
  COALESCE(array_length(p.pros, 1) > 0, false)                               AS has_pros,
  COALESCE(array_length(p.cons, 1) > 0, false)                               AS has_cons,
  COALESCE(p.seo_title IS NOT NULL AND p.seo_title <> '', false)             AS has_seo_title,
  COALESCE(p.seo_description IS NOT NULL AND p.seo_description <> '', false)  AS has_seo_description
FROM (SELECT $1::text AS slug) q
LEFT JOIN products p ON p.slug = q.slug;

nodes.name: Check Existing AI Fields
nodes.notes: v3.2: query rewritten to ALWAYS return exactly one row (single-row seed LEFT JOIN products) so new products no longer halt the loop here. found=false + has_*=false for new products → Compute AI Gate sets skipAi=false → AI runs. alwaysOutputData:true is an extra safety net.

nodes.name: Compute AI Gate
nodes.notes: NEW: Merges DB-populated check + overrideAiFields flag into a single skipAi boolean.

nodes.name: AI Fields Gate
nodes.notes: NEW: true-output (skipAi=false) → AI enrichment branch. false-output (skipAi=true) → bypass branch.

nodes.name: Build AI Enrichment Request
nodes.notes: NEW: Sends available subcategory names in prompt so AI picks an exact match. pros/cons requested as arrays, not strings.

nodes.name: AI Rate Limit (Enrichment)
nodes.notes: v3: Proactively spaces OpenRouter requests by aiRateLimitSeconds (Config). Sits before the AI enrichment call. retryOnFail on the HTTP node still handles 429/transient errors reactively.

nodes.name: Call OpenRouter (AI Enrichment)
nodes.notes: v3.3: Authorization injected by the predefined 'OpenRouter API' credential (paste API key only — n8n adds 'Bearer <key>'). You MUST create this credential and SELECT it on this node after import, else OpenRouter returns 401 'No auth credentials found' and pros/cons/seo come back empty. neverError:true captures 429/4xx bodies (now surfaced by Parse & Validate). 4 retries @ 3 s.

nodes.name: Parse & Validate AI Fields
nodes.notes: NEW: pros/cons returned as string[] (text[] compatible). subcategory_id resolved via 3-tier fuzzy match against Fetch Subcategories rows. seo_title ≤60, seo_description ≤160 hard-enforced.

nodes.name: AI Skip (already populated)
nodes.notes: v3.2: False-branch of AI Fields Gate. Passes product through with existing DB values untouched (empty pros/cons/seo; the upsert's COALESCE/non-empty guards preserve DB values). Connects directly to Build Classifier Request.

nodes.name: AI Rate Limit (Classifier)
nodes.notes: v3: Proactively spaces the second OpenRouter request (category classifier) by aiRateLimitSeconds (Config).

nodes.name: Call OpenRouter
nodes.notes: v3.3: Authorization injected by the predefined 'OpenRouter API' credential (same one as the enrichment node — select it here too). Category classifier call. On 401 it falls back to defaultCategoryId.

nodes.name: Upload to S3
nodes.notes: v3: REPLACES the hand-rolled SigV4 Code node + HTTP PUT pair. Uploads the downloaded image binary (property `data`) to the S3-compatible bucket using the '4everland S3' credential — endpoint (https://endpoint.4everland.co), region, access key, and secret key all live in n8n's credentials manager. Object key = Resolve Category.imageKey (e.g. products/amazon/<ASIN>/main.jpg). If 4everland rejects virtual-host addressing, enable 'Force Path Style' on the credential. Fallback documented in n8n/README.md if the native node can't reach the endpoint.

parameters.jsCode:
// Build Payload (eBay) — assembles the upsert JSON blob and the price-snapshot args.
const cfg = $('Config').first().json;
const resolved = $('Resolve Category').first().json;
const product = resolved.product;

const storedImage = { key: resolved.imageKey, alt: resolved.imageAlt, width: resolved.imageDim, height: resolved.imageDim };
const seo_og_image = { key: resolved.imageKey, alt: resolved.imageAlt, width: resolved.imageDim, height: resolved.imageDim };

const fullPayload = {
  slug: product.slug,
  title: product.title,
  brand: product.brand,
  short_description: product.short_description,
  description: product.description,
  category_id: product.category_id,
  price: product.price,
  original_price: product.original_price ?? null,
  currency: product.currency,
  discount_percent: product.discount_percent,
  deal_label: product.deal_label ?? null,
  affiliate_source: product.affiliate_source, // 'aliexpress'
  amazon_affiliate_tag: product.amazon_affiliate_tag ?? null,
  affiliate_url: product.affiliate_url,
  seller: product.seller ?? null,
  condition: product.condition ?? null,
  rating: product.rating,
  features: Array.isArray(product.features) ? product.features : [],
  tags: Array.isArray(product.tags) ? product.tags : [],
  pros: Array.isArray(product.pros) ? product.pros : [],
  cons: Array.isArray(product.cons) ? product.cons : [],
  images: [storedImage],
  seo_og_image,
  subcategory_id: product.subcategory_id ?? null,
  seo_title: product.seo_title ?? null,
  seo_description: product.seo_description ?? null,
  expires_at: product.expires_at ?? null,
};

console.log(JSON.stringify({
  event: 'payload_built',
  asin: resolved.asin,
  slug: product.slug,
  affiliate_url: product.affiliate_url,
  seller: product.seller ?? null,
  condition: product.condition ?? null,
  subcategory_id: product.subcategory_id ?? null,
  seo_title_len: (product.seo_title || '').length,
  seo_desc_len: (product.seo_description || '').length,
  pros_count: (product.pros || []).length,
  cons_count: (product.cons || []).length,
  aiStatus: resolved._aiStatus || 'n/a',
  classifier: resolved._classifier,
  ts: new Date().toISOString(),
}));

return [{
  json: {
    payloadJson: JSON.stringify(fullPayload),
    defaultCategoryId: cfg.defaultCategoryId,
    // Price-snapshot args (read by Record Price Snapshot):
    snapPrice: product.price,
    snapOriginal: product.original_price ?? null,
    snapCurrency: product.currency,
    snapDiscount: product.discount_percent,
    _classifier: resolved._classifier,
    _aiStatus: resolved._aiStatus || 'n/a',
  },
}];

nodes.name: Build Payload
nodes.notes: Assembles the upsert blob (incl. AliExpress seller, condition, expires_at) and carries snapshot args (price/original/currency/discount) for Record Price Snapshot. All column names match the products table exactly.

parameters.query:
-- Upsert Product (AliExpress). Single parameterised JSONB blob ($1) + defaultCategoryId ($2).
-- Adds seller, "condition" (quoted — column name), expires_at to the Amazon mapping.
WITH input AS (
  SELECT * FROM jsonb_to_record($1::jsonb) AS r(
    slug                 text,
    title                text,
    brand                text,
    short_description    text,
    description          text,
    category_id          uuid,
    price                numeric,
    original_price       numeric,
    currency             text,
    discount_percent     int,
    deal_label           text,
    affiliate_source     text,
    amazon_affiliate_tag text,
    affiliate_url        text,
    seller               text,
    "condition"          text,
    rating               real,
    images               jsonb,
    features             jsonb,
    tags                 jsonb,
    pros                 jsonb,
    cons                 jsonb,
    subcategory_id       uuid,
    seo_title            text,
    seo_description      text,
    seo_og_image         jsonb,
    expires_at           text
  )
)
INSERT INTO products (
  slug, title, brand, short_description, description,
  category_id, price, original_price, currency, discount_percent,
  deal_label, is_deal, affiliate_source, amazon_affiliate_tag, affiliate_url,
  seller, "condition",
  rating, images, features, tags, pros, cons,
  subcategory_id, seo_title, seo_description, seo_og_image,
  expires_at, published_at
)
SELECT
  slug, title, brand, short_description, description,
  category_id,
  price::numeric(10,2),
  original_price::numeric(10,2),
  currency::public.currency,
  discount_percent,
  deal_label,
  true,
  affiliate_source::public.affiliate_source,
  amazon_affiliate_tag,
  affiliate_url,
  seller,
  "condition",
  rating,
  images,
  ARRAY(SELECT jsonb_array_elements_text(features)) AS features,
  ARRAY(SELECT jsonb_array_elements_text(tags))     AS tags,
  ARRAY(SELECT jsonb_array_elements_text(pros))     AS pros,
  ARRAY(SELECT jsonb_array_elements_text(cons))     AS cons,
  subcategory_id,
  left(seo_title, 60)        AS seo_title,
  left(seo_description, 160) AS seo_description,
  seo_og_image,
  nullif(expires_at, '')::timestamptz AS expires_at,
  NOW() AS published_at
FROM input
ON CONFLICT (slug) DO UPDATE SET
  title                = EXCLUDED.title,
  brand                = EXCLUDED.brand,
  short_description    = EXCLUDED.short_description,
  description          = EXCLUDED.description,
  price                = EXCLUDED.price,
  original_price       = EXCLUDED.original_price,
  currency             = EXCLUDED.currency,
  discount_percent     = EXCLUDED.discount_percent,
  deal_label           = EXCLUDED.deal_label,
  is_deal              = true,
  affiliate_source     = EXCLUDED.affiliate_source,
  affiliate_url        = EXCLUDED.affiliate_url,
  amazon_affiliate_tag = EXCLUDED.amazon_affiliate_tag,
  seller               = EXCLUDED.seller,
  "condition"          = EXCLUDED."condition",
  rating               = EXCLUDED.rating,
  expires_at           = EXCLUDED.expires_at,
  images               = CASE WHEN jsonb_array_length(EXCLUDED.images) > 0
                               THEN EXCLUDED.images ELSE products.images END,
  seo_og_image         = COALESCE(EXCLUDED.seo_og_image, products.seo_og_image),
  features             = CASE WHEN array_length(EXCLUDED.features, 1) > 0
                               THEN EXCLUDED.features ELSE products.features END,
  tags                 = CASE WHEN array_length(EXCLUDED.tags, 1) > 0
                               THEN EXCLUDED.tags ELSE products.tags END,
  pros                 = CASE WHEN array_length(EXCLUDED.pros, 1) > 0
                               THEN EXCLUDED.pros ELSE products.pros END,
  cons                 = CASE WHEN array_length(EXCLUDED.cons, 1) > 0
                               THEN EXCLUDED.cons ELSE products.cons END,
  category_id          = CASE WHEN products.category_id = $2::uuid
                               THEN EXCLUDED.category_id ELSE products.category_id END,
  subcategory_id       = COALESCE(EXCLUDED.subcategory_id, products.subcategory_id),
  seo_title            = COALESCE(EXCLUDED.seo_title, products.seo_title),
  seo_description      = COALESCE(EXCLUDED.seo_description, products.seo_description),
  updated_at           = NOW()
RETURNING id, slug, (xmax = 0) AS was_inserted;

nodes.name: Upsert Product
nodes.notes: INSERT … ON CONFLICT (slug). Includes seller, "condition" (quoted column), expires_at. affiliate_source cast accepts 'aliexpress'. COALESCE / non-empty guards preserve AI fields on skipped-AI re-runs.

nodes.name: Audit Log
nodes.notes: NEW: Structured audit log entry after every upsert. Extend to POST to a webhook / Datadog / Slack.

parameters.query:
-- Record a price_snapshots row only on first sight or when price/discount changed,
-- keyed by the product id returned from the upsert. alwaysOutputData keeps the loop alive
-- when no row is inserted (0-row Postgres output would otherwise stall splitInBatches).
WITH latest AS (
  SELECT price, discount_percent FROM price_snapshots
  WHERE product_sanity_id = $1::uuid ORDER BY captured_at DESC LIMIT 1
)
INSERT INTO price_snapshots (product_sanity_id, price, original_price, currency, discount_percent)
SELECT $1::uuid, $2::numeric(10,2), $3::numeric(10,2), $4::text, $5::int
WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM latest)
   OR (SELECT price FROM latest) <> $2::numeric(10,2)
   OR COALESCE((SELECT discount_percent FROM latest), -1) <> $5::int
RETURNING id;

nodes.name: Record Price Snapshot
nodes.notes: Version tracking (#4): appends a price_snapshots row only when first seen or price/discount changed. alwaysOutputData:true so a no-insert (unchanged price) still emits an item and the loop continues to Throttle.

nodes.name: Throttle
nodes.notes: Configurable gap between ASIN iterations → back to Loop Over Items.

nodes.name: Error Trigger
nodes.notes: Fires when an execution of this workflow fails. In n8n Settings → set this workflow as its own 'Error Workflow' (or assign it globally) so unhandled failures (token error, 0 deals discovered, upsert/S3 failure after retries) reach Send Alert.

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ (() => { const e=$json||{}; const wf=(e.workflow&&e.workflow.name)||'AliExpress Deals → Postgres'; const ex=e.execution||{}; const node=ex.lastNodeExecuted||'unknown'; const msg=(ex.error&&ex.error.message)||'unknown error'; const text='🚨 ' + wf + ' failed at node "' + node + '": ' + msg; return JSON.stringify({ text, content: text, executionId: ex.id||null, ts: new Date().toISOString() }); })() }}

nodes.name: Send Alert
nodes.notes: Critical-failure alert (#5). PASTE your Slack/Discord/generic webhook URL above — the Error Trigger runs in a separate execution where $('Config') and $env are unavailable, so the URL can't be read from Config. Body sends both 'text' (Slack) and 'content' (Discord). neverError so the alert never masks the original failure.

nodes.name: Search AliExpress Hot Products
nodes.notes: Signed aliexpress.affiliate.hotproduct.query (replaces eBay OAuth + Browse search + Extract Items). Signs in-node with crypto (requires NODE_FUNCTION_ALLOW_BUILTIN=crypto), dedups by product_id, caps at maxDealsPerRun, throws (→ Error Trigger → Send Alert) on signature/permission/empty-result failures. The summary already carries promotion_link/price/image.

nodes.name: Fetch Product Detail
nodes.notes: Signed aliexpress.affiliate.productdetail.get for the current product (replaces eBay Fetch Item Detail). SOFT-FAILS to the hotproduct.query summary if the detail call errors, so the loop never stalls.
